definitions.technical: A formal statement issued by an independent accounting firm confirming that a stablecoin issuer's reserves meet the composition and valuation standards required by the GENIUS Act. Attestations are required on a monthly cycle.
definitions.lay: An official report from an outside accounting firm that checks whether a stablecoin issuer actually holds enough qualifying assets to back all the tokens it has issued. The GENIUS Act requires this check every month.
definitions.parenthetical: (an independent accountant's formal confirmation that reserve assets meet statutory requirements)
definitions.legal_significance: The GENIUS Act mandates monthly attestations as a core prudential safeguard. The comment letter argues that principles-based diversification standards (Option A) cannot be reliably audited through this attestation process.
